sections: What a White-Glove Move Actually Involves

A white-glove move is defined by what you don’t have to do. From the first survey to the moment the last picture is levelled on the wall, our team carries the entire weight of the relocation so you can carry on with your life, your work and your family. There are no part-packed rooms left for you to finish, no flat-pack puzzles waiting in the new hallway and no boxes you are expected to wrestle yourself. Our white-glove crews handle planning, protection, packing, transport, unpacking and final placement as one seamless, supervised operation.

As a family-run Sussex removals & storage company founded in 2016, and a member of LAPADA — the Association of Art & Antiques Dealers — we built this service for households where time is scarce and possessions are precious. Every white-glove booking is led by a named move coordinator who knows your floor plan, your timings and the items that need particular care. If you want to see how the fully managed approach fits your move, you can request a tailored white-glove quote and we’ll build the plan around you.
The Concierge Process, Step by Step

Precision is what separates a premium relocation from an ordinary one. Our white-glove service follows a structured, end-to-end process so that nothing is improvised on the day. It begins with a detailed home survey — in person or by video — where we record room dimensions, access constraints, parking, stairwells and the inventory of high-value pieces. From there we produce a written move plan covering crating, packing materials, vehicle allocation, crew size and a room-by-room placement schedule for the destination.

On packing day, our fully trained teams arrive with archival tissue, acid-free wrap, custom cartons and purpose-built protection. Each phase is logged and supervised so the move stays on schedule and within scope.

  • Pre-move survey, inventory and a written, room-by-room plan
  • Professional full packing of every room using premium materials
  • Bespoke protection for valuables, including a custom crate service for the most delicate items
  • Loading, secure transport and careful unloading by an experienced crew
  • Complete unpacking and placement with debris and packaging removed
Handling, Crating & Placement of High-Value Items

The heart of any white-glove relocation is the handling of things that cannot simply be replaced. Original artwork, gilt-framed mirrors, marble-topped consoles, grandfather clocks, chandeliers, ceramics and inherited furniture all demand more than ordinary blankets and tape. Our LAPADA membership reflects a genuine specialism in this area, and our crews apply museum-style handling principles to private homes: clean cotton gloves, two-person lifts, corner and edge protection, and never resting a frame face-down or a leg under load.

Where a piece is irreplaceable or awkwardly shaped, we build protection around it rather than forcing it into a standard box. That might mean a bespoke timber crate, a soft-pack suspension wrap or a foam-lined case sized to the object. For dedicated antique handling we draw on our specialised antiques moving team, and for glass, porcelain and art we apply the same techniques used in our fragile packing service. Final placement is just as deliberate — we hang, level, position and reassemble so the room is finished, not just emptied of boxes.

White-glove standards don’t only apply to home moves. We regularly support interior designers, antiques dealers, galleries and private clients with the careful collection, transport and installation of individual pieces. A single statement chandelier, a commissioned dining table or a delicate console destined for a styled room all benefit from the same protected handling and discreet, on-time delivery we bring to a full house move.

For trade clients who need a dependable, repeatable standard, we offer scheduled contract delivery services across the South East, ensuring each item arrives wrapped, undamaged and placed exactly where the brief requires. Whether it is a one-off showroom installation or a recurring designer programme, the watchwords stay the same: protection, punctuality and a clean, finished result with no packaging left behind.

  • Single-item collection, crating and protected transport
  • Showroom and gallery installation with placement to a brief
  • Recurring trade and designer delivery schedules
  • Coverage across West & East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent
Discretion, Insurance & Who the Service Suits

Many of our white-glove clients value privacy as much as care. Our crews work quietly, dress and behave professionally and never discuss a client’s home, address or contents. Vehicles can be parked discreetly, timings arranged around your schedule and the whole move handled with the minimum of disruption to neighbours or staff. Because we are Checkatrade-verified and fully insured with £10m liability cover, you have the reassurance of an accountable, independently reviewed company standing behind every booking.

This service suits busy professionals who simply cannot give days to a move, owners of luxury and period homes where the contents warrant specialist handling, and those downsizing from a larger property who want their most treasured possessions treated with respect rather than rushed. It also suits anyone relocating long-distance — we move clients across the UK and into Europe — who wants a single, managed point of contact from start to finish. To understand the experience and credentials behind the service, you can read more about our family-run Sussex team.

We are also frequently recommended by estate agents whose clients expect a higher standard, including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart. That reputation has been earned move by move, never by cutting corners. When you book a white-glove relocation you are buying back your own time and handing the detail to a team that has packed, crated, transported and placed countless precious collections without drama. The result on completion day is a home that already feels lived-in: beds made up, pictures hung, kitchens unpacked and the last of the cartons gone.
faqs: What is included in your white-glove moving service?

The white-glove service is fully managed end to end. It includes a pre-move survey and written plan, full packing of every room with premium materials, bespoke protection and crating for valuables, careful loading and transport, then complete unpacking, furniture reassembly, final placement and the removal of all packaging. You do nothing except tell us where things should go.
Do you handle art, antiques and other high-value items?

Yes. As a LAPADA member we specialise in handling fine art, antiques, mirrors, clocks, chandeliers and other irreplaceable pieces. We use cotton gloves, two-person lifts and tailored protection, and for the most delicate items we build a custom crate or use our specialised antiques moving team rather than a standard box.
Can you deliver single items to designers or showrooms?

Absolutely. We support interior designers, dealers and private clients with protected collection, transport and on-site installation of individual pieces, as well as recurring contract deliveries. Each item arrives wrapped, undamaged and placed to your brief, with packaging cleared away afterwards.
Which areas do you cover and how do I book?

We are based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough RH20 3JT, and cover West & East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, plus moves across the UK and into Europe. To arrange a white-glove move, call us on 01903 893731, email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk or request a quote online and we’ll build a plan around your home and timings.
